source.subsection:
## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/nojdvjz2/ustvolskaya-symphonies.png)**

## **Ustvolskaya** Symphonies

**London Philharmonic Orchestra / Christian Karlsen**(BIS)

These five symphonies offer us a fascinating and superbly performed insight into the music of the 20th-century Russian composer Galina Ustvolskaya.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/ustvolskaya-symphonies-nos-1-5-karlsen)

source.subsection:
## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/hlrkoe4d/whispers-thunder.jpg)**

## **‘Whispers & Thunder’**

**Illia Ovcharenko** _pf_(Steinway & Sons)

Wide-ranging repertoire – Revutsky, Scarlatti, Silvestrov and Liszt’s mighty B minor Sonata – reveal what an imaginative player is this month’s One to Watch, pianist Illia Ovcharenko.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/illia-ovcharenko-whispers-and-thunder)

source.subsection:
## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/r4ggnv2t/debussy-chamber-works-the-nash-ensemble.jpg)**

## **Debussy** Chamber Works

**The Nash Ensemble**(Hyperion)

A gorgeous immersion in Debussy’s music from the excellent players that comprise the Nash Ensemble – in fact it’s just as much an immersion in their chamber music-making brilliance.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/debussy-string-quartet-sonatas-the-nash-ensemble)
